title: Kalman Filter State Estimation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Kalman Filter State Estimation: Kalman filter: optimal linear estimator for Gaussian linear dynamics; minimizes mean squared error of state estimate. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.kalman.predict_update…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~20; Q_R_mismatch, model_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: x_{k+1} = A*x_k + B*u_k + w_k, w~N(0,Q); y_k = C*x_k + v_k, v~N(0,R)
E.world_state_x: posterior state estimate x_hat_k|k and covariance P_k|k

W.regime: Existence of the recovered state_estimate_covariance is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 20); Q_R_mismatch dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: NEES ~ chi2(n)/n in expectation for consistent filter; NIS ~ chi2(p)/p. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~20. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |Q_R_mismatch|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n ** 2 * p) per update step per iteration
